label: Thicker prerequisite ramp; show cause + takeaway

changelog.summary: From Madrona reader feedback: a prerequisite-gap ramp gives a refresher scene to every prerequisite the paper needs that the audience doesn't already know (bedrock nouns of the paper's field — agent, simulator, GPU/warp — count as undefined terms for an out-of-field reader). Scenes must show the cause of an effect, not just the effect, and make their single takeaway unmistakable; the reviewer now fails scenes whose takeaway is unclear or that show an effect without its cause.
List abstract tightened to 2-3 short sentences (down from 2-4), enforced by a deterministic sentence-count cap in the prose reviewer. The explainer-top hook stays a separate, motivational lead-in.

Clear-writing rules from plain-language research: the abstract is 2-4 short sentences (one idea each, ~15-20 words, headline number on its own line), never a run-on. The prose reviewer now also evaluates the abstract, and a deterministic check flags any sentence over 30 words across the abstract, hook, and captions.

The list gist (oneSentenceClaim) must read like a plain-language abstract, not a dense claim. The hook must be sensible English with no slop, and any metaphor must be genuinely idiomatic (no invented images like 'a fort out of movable boxes'). Added a prose reviewer that reads the hook + captions before graphics and auto-revises slop / non-idiomatic metaphors / undefined terms; the scene reviewer also fails caption slop.

Visuals carry the explanation: every scene is a labeled diagram (structure) or an interactive chart with the independent variable on a reader-moved control (relationships). Captions cut from 3-5 sentences to 1-2 that point at the visual. Metaphor is a doorway, not a residence (introduce, then name the real term and drop the metaphor). Removed the story-roles rule.

Feynman-first four-part arc (building blocks -> new tech -> why it beats existing -> why now). 3-5 sentence captions in Feynman voice with Zinsser economy. Introduce-then-use vocabulary; sustained metaphors with explicit mappings; story roles over algebra labels; honest black boxes for beyond-audience mechanisms.
